extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won a technical finish sprint in Toulouse, latching onto teammate GROENEWEGEN Dylan's wheel after a crash inside 10km.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma committed to dual-sprinter leadout, Teunissen's work enabled Ewan to parasitically overtake Groenewegen at line.
Crash at 31km delayed Porte and Quintana, revealing vulnerability of GC riders without strong positioning squads.
Degenkolb attacked solo with 25s gap under 10km, showing combativity prize hunting overrides breakaway cooperation dynamics.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: crashed inside 10km, regained peloton, then still won—exceptional recovery capacity and positioning instinct
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: delivered perfect leadout but lost to own teammate Ewan, suggesting inferior final 200m kick
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: negative: dropped time at team car, fell out of top 10 GC on flat stage—Trek's race management questionable
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: caught in 31km crash while 8th GC at 2:04, demonstrates persistent positioning weakness
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: caught in second crash after stage 10 crosswind losses, pattern of bad positioning continues
